other_observers: None; This bird was first found on the Penrose CBC , 12/16/2012 by Dan Maynard at a location along Willow road, Canyon City.
specific_location: Centennial Park , near the open pond.
description: Same size and shape of adjacent American Wigeon.Reddish brown head and neck , cream crown , gray back and flanks , black tail make this ID straightfoward.Behaviors: Feeding on short grass in park in accompany with 75 American Wigeon. Did not join the AMWI attracted to the pond being fed by people but joined a group of 6 AMWI which to flight and departed east along the Arkansas River.Appeared wild and wary , no sign of leg bands or wing damage.Call: None heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond
